arts.ttl: How to watch Newcastle United v Bromley on TV today - live stream details
arts.sum: This is Bromley's first-ever FA Cup third-round tie

arts.ttl: Newcastle United drop two early team news hints for Bromley as Eddie Howe faces FA Cup decision
arts.sum: With the hectic fixture schedule and playing a lower league opposition, the game could give Howe the chance to rotate his team, and give players who may have struggled for game time this season more minutes. In the last three Premier League games he has played just four minutes, although he did come on for the final 25 minutes of the Carabao Cup win over Arsenal.

arts.ttl: Newcastle United star Martin Dubravka breaks silence as Saudi transfer deadline set
arts.sum: But as Chronicle Live reported on Friday, Howe said even if Dubravka stayed and played a couple more times this season that would be deemed the "right decision" from a football point of view - especially if he helps Newcastle book their place in the Carabao Cup final should Nick Pope's injury issues continue. As Chronicle Live reported on Saturday, Dubravka had been given an ultimatum from Saudi Pro League club Al-Shabab after they set a weekend deadline for him to make up his mind on a January move.

arts.ttl: Newcastle vs Bromley Prediction: Team News, Predicted Lineups and Where To Watch
arts.sum: Newcastle host League Two side Bromley in the FA Cup this afternoon. Ahead of the clash, we’ve made a prediction for the game, provided our predicted line-ups for both teams, and picked out a B...

arts.ttl: I know how Alexander Isak will be feeling right now and it doesn't get any better
arts.sum: He will sit out today's game against Bromley in the FA Cup, but will be raring to go when Wolves come to St James' Park for Wendesday night's league clash - and Shearer feels his feelgood factor is sweeping through the dressing room. "Not only does he have that feeling right now, but the team does as well because of the way that they’re playing.

arts.ttl: Newcastle vs Bromley: Preview, predictions and lineups
arts.sum: Newcastle United are searching for an eighth successive victory in all competitions when they host League Two outfit Bromley in the FA Cup third round on Sunday afternoon. Given their recent scalps, a visit from fourth tier Bromley seems rather straightforward for Newcastle, but the minnows will be aiming to conjure some of the FA Cup's fabled magic during their first ever third round fixture.

arts.ttl: Newcastle United make Sean Longstaff decision following 'very difficult' choice
arts.sum: Howe's move to play Sandro Tonali at number 6 and Bruno Guimaraes at number 8 has had major consequences for Longstaff with the midfielder restricted to cameos from the bench in recent weeks. When asked about Longstaff's position, Howe told Chronicle Live: "I think Sean is fighting for his place and he has responded well to that challenge.

arts.ttl: Bruno return, Trippier role, Miley chance - Newcastle United starting XI calls made vs Bromley - Lee Ryder - Chronicle Live
arts.sum: My predicted NUFC XI: Vlachodimos, Trippier (c), Schar, Kelly, Targett, Longstaff, Willock, Miley, Almiron, Barnes, Osula My predicted NUFC XI: Vlachodimos, Trippier, Schar, Kelly, Targett, Bruno, Longstaff, Miley, Almiron, Osula, Barnes

arts.ttl: Bromley goalkeeper wants to dump Newcastle out of FA Cup: 'Why shouldn't it be us'
arts.sum: Grant Smith helped Bromley reach the EFL for the first time in their history last season and now has eyes on causing a mighty FA Cup upset when the League Two side travel to Newcastle

arts.ttl: Newcastle now on Khvicha Kvaratskhelia transfer red alert after shock Antonio Conte statement
arts.sum: Napoli manager Antonio Conte has revealed Newcastle United-linked Khvicha Kvaratskhelia has asked to leave the club. "Quality is needed in addition to the work I demanded, as well as the confirmation of certain players, including Khvicha, who, as you know, had asked to be sold in the summer.
